events.title: Three Years for the King
events.scene: The Kingdom of Bavaria wants three years of your body for its army, and the letter has come. A wine village in the Palatinate offers a life you can already see to its end. The boat to the new world offers everything and nothing. To carry the {surname} name forward, you must decide whether your future is here, in uniform, or across an ocean you have never seen.
events.researchNote: Place-specific Bavarian origins beat (EX-2): conscription-dodging emigration from the Palatinate wine country was a classic 19th-c. German-immigrant origin. Generic to any Bavarian founding.

choices.text: Leave a letter for your mother and walk to the boat by night
choices.outcome: You slip away before dawn with the barber's savings in your pocket. The {surname} line will be made in a country that does not yet know your name.

choices.text: Report for the uniform and earn your standing the respectable way
choices.outcome: You drill in Bavarian mud for three years and muster out a respectable veteran. The {surname} name stays in the Palatinate, solid and small.

choices.text: Master a trade — barber, brewer — that will travel anywhere
choices.outcome: You learn a craft a man can carry across any border. Whether you leave or stay, the {surname} line will never want for a living.

events.title: Wine, Hops, and the Old Country's Craft
events.scene: Your village runs on the vine and the brew-kettle, and the knowledge is in your hands as surely as the Rhine runs through the valley. That craft is the {surname} line's one transportable fortune — worth little here among a hundred other brewers, worth a great deal somewhere thirsty and far away. The question is what you make of it.
events.researchNote: The Palatinate's wine/brewing culture as the line's inheritable trade — a Bavarian-specific founding lever (and the seed of a later bootlegging pivot under Prohibition).

choices.text: Carry the brewing craft to a thirsty new market
choices.outcome: You pack the old country's craft like a dowry. Where you land, the {surname} kettle will turn grain into gold faster than any rival's.

choices.text: Keep the vineyard and the name where they have always been
choices.outcome: The {surname} vines stay rooted in Palatinate chalk. A smaller fortune, but one that does not weep for a home left behind.

events.title: A German Name in a Hostile Year
events.scene: The wider world has turned against everything Prussian and Bavarian alike, and your old-country name draws sideways looks on every street. The {surname} line must decide how to weather suspicion: wear the name proudly, soften it, or bury it under a more palatable story.
events.researchNote: Anti-German sentiment (WWI-era) pressuring immigrant families to anglicize/obscure origins — a Bavarian-line trope that recurs as the 'old country problem' in later eras.

choices.text: Wear the {surname} name proudly — the old country is craft and order, not shame
choices.outcome: You refuse to flinch. It costs you a few customers and earns you a few enemies, but the {surname} name stands undisguised.

choices.text: Soften the story — claim a safer origin until the suspicion passes
choices.outcome: You let people believe a more convenient tale. The {surname} line keeps its custom — and a secret that will resurface a generation later.
